brand.name: MaxTracker
brand.description: An independent unofficial alternative to search TGV Max 0€ tickets, using SNCF Open Data API.

headings.description: Used for all H1-H6, delivering a modern, geometric, and highly legible tech feel.

body.description: Clean, highly readable geometric sans for dense UI details.

mono.description: For times, train numbers, and sync timestamps to ensure perfect vertical alignment.

primary.description: Deep Navy for primary buttons and strong trust-building elements.

accent.description: Vibrant Mint strictly used for '0€' highlights and success states.

secondary.description: Soft slate for secondary buttons, filters, and card backgrounds.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous spacing. Strict grid alignment for the results list.

header.classes: sticky top-0 z-50 backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/80 border-b border-slate-200

hero_search.layout: Bento-style overlapping search box over the hero background.
hero_search.input_classes: h-14 text-lg border-2 border-slate-200 rounded-lg focus:border-primary focus:ring-primary

filters.style: Pill-shaped toggles and Neumorphic active states.

train_card.layout: Flex row on desktop, stacked on mobile. Clear separation between departure/arrival times, destination, and the 0€ CTA.

disclaimer.classes: text-xs text-slate-500 bg-slate-100 p-4 rounded-lg mt-12

image_urls.description: Abstract sleek background with subtle light trails and deep blue tones, representing speed and travel.
Whimsical minimalist train station bench empty state illustration.

icons.usage: Use thick/bold icons (stroke-width: 2.5) for navigation and thin icons (stroke-width: 1.5) for train details.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: All buttons MUST have hover states with color shifts (e.g., bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90).
motion_and_interactions.page_load: Staggered fade-in for train results (framer-motion or simple CSS animation).
motion_and_interactions.loading: Use pulse animations (skeleton loaders) instead of spinners for data fetching.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure white text on accent mint (#10B981) meets 3:1 contrast for large buttons. If not, use darker mint or dark text.
accessibility.test_ids: All interactive elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='search-input', data-testid='swap-stations-btn', data-testid='train-card-cta').

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and configure 'Outfit' and 'DM Sans' fonts via Google Fonts in index.html or layout.
Use Shadcn UI for standard inputs (Input, Select, Switch for filters, Button, Card, Skeleton).
Build the Calendar view either using a custom CSS Grid or integrating a simple Shadcn Calendar if it fits.
Build a custom Recharts bar chart for the 'Peak hours probability graph'.
Ensure 'Départ imminent' (<4h) badge dynamically renders in the Train Card.
Ensure the hero background image has a subtle overlay (e.g., bg-black/30) if text is placed directly over it, or place the search form in a solid white Bento card floating over the image.
Do not use placeholders for missing images; rely purely on the URLs provided in the media section.
Create a responsive 12-column grid layout where the left 3 columns are for filters and the right 9 for the results feed.
